mappings.rationaleAI: Central article on the Aztec/Mexica civilization covering the Triple Alliance formation by 1427, military campaigns under Itzcoatl and Montezuma, altepetl city-state structure, chinampas agriculture, tribute and taxation, Huitzilopochtli worship, Templo Mayor architecture, ritual calendar, and Spanish conquest of 1521. Maps directly to C.2 (development and extension of Aztec rule) and C.3 (Aztec culture, society, agriculture, political organization, religion, art, and architecture).

Core survey article on the Mesoamerican cultural region covering Olmec foundations, Teotihuacan, Maya and Zapotec writing systems, calendrical systems, ritual ballgame, maize and cacao domestication, and the Aztec Triple Alliance. Directly covers A (character, achievements, and geography of Mesoamerican civilization) and broadly addresses B.1 (Pre-Classic development including Olmec) and B.2.a (Teotihuacan).

Covers the Maya from Preclassic agricultural origins through Classic-period city-states (Tikal, Calakmul), hieroglyphic script, Long Count calendar, divine kingship, the Classic Maya collapse (9th century), and Postclassic Chichén Itzá. Maps to B.1.d (Mayas of the Chicanel), B.2.a.ii (Tzakol/Tepeu lowland Maya), B.2.c (Late Classic Maya urban settlements, art, calendar, religion, and collapse), and C.1 (Toltec-Maya culture of Chichen Itza).

Covers Mexico's history of advanced pre-Columbian civilizations including Maya and Aztec/Mexica, Spanish conquest, and the deep embedding of Mesoamerican indigenous heritage in national culture. Relevant to A (geography and ethnography of Mesoamerica) and C.2–C.3 (Aztec state and culture).

Broad survey covering all Mesoamerican civilizations before European contact including Olmec (B.1.b–c), Maya (B.2.c), and Aztec (C.2–C.3), with attention to Pre-Classic, Classic, and Post-Classic periods. Maps to A (character and achievements) and the full chronological outline of B and C.

Covers the Spanish conquest of the Aztec Empire under Hernán Cortés (1519–1521) as a central event of the Age of Discovery, directly relevant to C.2 (administrative techniques under Montezuma II and the end of Aztec rule) and C.3 (Aztec society at the time of Spanish conquest).

Covers the Spanish conquest of the Aztec Empire and subsequent colonial administration, connecting to C.2 (Aztec state through the Spanish conquest) and C.3 (Aztec culture and society up to the time of the conquest and its immediate aftermath).

Covers pre-Columbian Indigenous civilizations in the Mesoamerican region (within the broader North American scope), relevant to A (character and geography of Mesoamerican civilization) and B.1 (Pre-Classic development including early Paleo-Indian and agricultural transitions in the region).

The Post-Classic period (c. 900–1450) in the Americas is addressed within this world history survey, covering political and cultural developments in Mesoamerica relevant to C.1 (Toltec state) and C.2 (Aztec state formation from c. 1325).
